channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Empowerms] U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home
item.description:
U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home In a time of growing global instability, the role of American strength is back at the center of the conversation. In this episode, Grant sits down with Roger Wicker, Chairman of the Senate Armed Services Committee and Mississippi’s senior senator. From rebuilding America’s military strength to…

The post U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Why New Jersey’s Small Businesses Deserve Better Policy
item.description:
by Megan Whitman, NJPI Jr. Fellow Walk down any main street in New Jersey and you’re looking at the heart […]

The post Why New Jersey’s Small Businesses Deserve Better Policy first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] Governor’s Paid Leave “Fix” Leaves the Biggest Problems Untouched
item.description:
Earlier this year, I warned that Virginia’s proposed Paid Family and Medical Leave Act would create one of the most expansive and expensive paid leave programs in the country. I had hoped the Governor’s new amendments issued just before midnight last night would fix those concerns. They don’t. The legislation would still establish a statewide insurance system funded by mandatory payroll contributions from workers […]

The post Governor’s Paid Leave “Fix” Leaves the Biggest Problems Untouched appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling
item.description:
The Law Centre today delivered a formal letter to Dr. Jerome Larkin, Director of the Rhode Island Department of Health (RIDOH), demanding that the agency immediately cease enforcement of Rhode Island’s ban on conversion therapy for minors. The action follows the U.S. Supreme Court’s March 21, 2026 decision in Chiles v. Salazar, which struck down Colorado’s similar law as a violation of the First Amendment.

The post Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] 2026 Green Book
item.description: 2026 Kansas Green Book – Exploring the relationship between the size of government and economic growth

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] Minnesota Parents, We’re About to Lose Out on $800 Million for Our Children
item.description:
By Zach Freimark and Sarah Hitchcock America First Policy Institute On July 4, 2025, Congress passed, on a bipartisan vote, a package of reconciliations, commonly referred to as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. Included in the bill was a provision that created the Education Freedom Tax Credit (EFTC), which funds elementary and secondary education […]

The post Minnesota Parents, We’re About to Lose Out on $800 Million for Our Children appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Sine Die
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Sine Die Lawmakers returned to Jackson expecting veto overrides, but what followed was one of the more unusual endings to a legislative session in recent memory. We unpack the final day’s twists, the political fallout, and close the book on the 2026 session.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Sine Die first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] “Tax Me More” Fund Not Seeing Much Action Despite Tax Hikers’ False Appeals to Patriotism
item.description:
Americans are allowed to voluntarily contribute money to the U.S. Treasury’s “tax me more” fund. But not much money is being donated despite the false appeals to “patriotism” peddled by tax hikers. In calling for tax increases, affluent Democrats and progressive activists try to assert that Americans opposed to such tax hikes are lacking in […]

The post “Tax Me More” Fund Not Seeing Much Action Despite Tax Hikers’ False Appeals to Patriotism appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Secretary of State Bellows Butchers Another Ballot Question
item.description:
In the fall, Maine voters will have the opportunity to vote on a citizen referendum aimed at protecting girls’ sports by requiring schools to separate athletic teams and certain facilities based on biological sex as recorded at birth. Before they ever get the chance to vote on the policy, however, there is a critically important […]

The post Secretary of State Bellows Butchers Another Ballot Question appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas Has a Cost Problem—And It’s Driving People Away
item.description: Kansas isn’t collapsing. That would at least get attention. What’s happening is quieter and more dangerous: the state is becoming too expensive, too fragmented, and too average to compete, while other states pull ahead. The new 2026 Kansas Green Book lays it out clearly. Kansas is not leading where it matters. It is lagging, and […]

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] Why Walz’s proposed social media tax is a bad idea
item.description:
It would run afoul of the First Amendment, among other issues. By Annette Meeks Guest contributor to the Minnesota Star Tribune April 20, 2026 ••• What do progressives do when they run out of other people’s money to spend? You don’t have to look any further than Minnesota to find out: Recently Gov. Tim Walz […]

The post Why Walz’s proposed social media tax is a bad idea appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Strengthening Her Family, Building Her Community: Shaneika Murphy’s Passion for Helping Others Flourish
item.description:
After completing the Raising Highly Capable Kids workshop as a parent, Shaneika Murphy now supports other parents as a program facilitator. Her sons volunteer with the program, too. Together, the Murphys are a shining example of how strong families build strong communities.

The post Strengthening Her Family, Building Her Community: Shaneika Murphy’s Passion for Helping Others Flourish appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Jbartlett] US public school spending hits $1 trillion; NH ranks 7th per-pupil
item.description:
Spending on K-12 public education in the United States passed the $1 trillion mark in the 2024 fiscal year, just-released data from the federal National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) reveal. “Time to update all mentions of the size of this sector,” commented the Edunomics Lab at Georgetown University’s McCourt School of Public Policy. “Revenues […]

The post US public school spending hits $1 trillion; NH ranks 7th per-pupil appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Beacontn] Positioning Tennessee to Be America’s Energy Leader
item.description: Tennessee’s rich nuclear history shows the state is a leader in nuclear energy and advanced technologies. With new technology and rising electricity demand, there are now opportunities for Tennessee to not only meet the energy needs of its growing population but also to be a model for the whole country in developing and deploying affordable, abundant energy.

item.title: [Empirecenter] New York’s Electricity Prices 70 Percent Above the National Average
item.description:
Recent data from the Energy Information Administration and Empire Center for Public Policy show New York’s average residential electricity prices....

The post New York’s Electricity Prices 70 Percent Above the National Average appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Raising Highly Capable Kids Program Increases Positive Values and Social Skills in Children
item.description:
GCO and its partners offered 15 RHCK workshops at 12 locations in 2025. Participants took pre- and post-workshop surveys that illustrated the ways families had grown stronger throughout the program.

The post Raising Highly Capable Kids Program Increases Positive Values and Social Skills in Children appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Empowerms] Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service
item.description:
Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service Mississippi Secretary of State Michael Watson returns to the Empower Podcast as he launches his campaign for Lt. Governor. In this conversation, Grant sits down with Watson for a more personal look at the experiences and convictions that have shaped his leadership. From growing up on the Gulf…

The post Michael Watson: Faith, Family, and Public Service first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] The NJEA Is Putting Politics Before Kids, Again
item.description:
New Jersey should opt into the Education Freedom Tax Credit. The union’s opposition tells you everything about whose interests it […]

The post The NJEA Is Putting Politics Before Kids, Again first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Buried Without a Vote: The Secret Power of Committee Chairmen
item.description: It isn’t a power afforded in House or Senate rules. It isn’t written into Mason’s Manual of Legislative Procedure, and it certainly isn’t penned into the Constitution. Yet, during legislative sessions at the Idaho Capitol, it is utilized and treated as an authorized exercise of power.  We are, of course, talking about the chairman’s drawer. […]

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Let’s Pack the U.S. House GALLUP REPORTS this week that the approval rate of Congress has fallen to 10 percent. Coincidentally, a Butler graduate student, Evan Wareham, has an intriguing idea — reapportionment on steroids. What if we reformed the U.S. House of Representatives by adding members to increase per-capita representation? That, please know, was the original plan. In Read the full article...

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: ALEC President Jonathan Williams on Nebraska’s Latest Ranking
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Jonathan Williams, co-author of Rich States, Poor States and President and Chief Economist at the American Legislative Exchange Council. During the episode Jonathan and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss the latest rankings in new edition of Rich States, Poor States, how Nebraska can improve,...

The post Nebraskanomics: ALEC President Jonathan Williams on Nebraska’s Latest Ranking appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF calls out City of Albuquerque for wasteful turf spending in KOAT Channel 7 story
item.description:
A few years ago the City of Albuquerque spent $235,000 for turf (in Rio Rancho) for the Duke City Gladiators. The team folded and a new arena team by a different name now plays at State owned Tingley Coliseum. What happened to the old turf? Let’s just say it has been “repurposed.” As the City […]

The post RGF calls out City of Albuquerque for wasteful turf spending in KOAT Channel 7 story appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Sales Tax Modernization Could Help Nebraska Stay Competitive
item.description:
In an increasingly competitive environment, states are competing every day for workers, entrepreneurs, investment, and new residents. Tax policy plays a major role in those decisions. States with more competitive tax structures are often better positioned to attract business investment, encourage job creation, and retain families looking for opportunity.  For Nebraska, that matters. Neighboring states...

The post Sales Tax Modernization Could Help Nebraska Stay Competitive appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF Petitions US Supreme Court to Protect Free Speech
item.description:
Thanks to our friends at the Liberty Justice Center the Rio Grande Foundation is appealing for certiorari (an appeal for our case to be heard) against New Mexico Secretary of State Maggie Toulouse Oliver. The case is called: Rio Grande Foundation v. Oliver. The case is based on the proposition that Americans have a constitutional […]

The post RGF Petitions US Supreme Court to Protect Free Speech appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] 2026 Legislative Wrap-Up
item.description:
As the 132nd Maine Legislature raced toward adjournment, lawmakers pushed through a wave of major bills in just a matter of days. The final stretch of the session was fast-moving, and decisions were made that will shape Maine’s economy, healthcare system, and tax climate for years to come. Maine Policy Institute closely tracked many of […]

The post 2026 Legislative Wrap-Up appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF in Las Cruces Sun News: Las Cruces overspent. It’s time to cut | Opinion
item.description:
The following appeared in the Las Cruces Sun-News on April 26, 2026. After years of overspending the City of Las Cruces, like Albuquerque, faces a budget deficit.  Unlike Albuquerque which has lost people, Las Cruces continues to grow and has seen a 7.08% increase since the 2020 census. That means that the City can and should be able to […]

The post RGF in Las Cruces Sun News: Las Cruces overspent. It’s time to cut | Opinion appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH’s low class sizes continue to shrink as enrollment falls faster than teacher rolls
item.description:
New Hampshire public school enrollment continues to decline at a faster rate than teacher employment, a Josiah Bartlett Center for Public Policy analysis of state data shows. Over the last 15 years, public schools in New Hampshire lost students at nearly three times the rate at which they lost teachers. From the 2011-2012 school year […]

The post NH’s low class sizes continue to shrink as enrollment falls faster than teacher rolls appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Saving Money Should Not Be a Thankless Task for Legislators
item.description: Not too long ago, I had a conversation with a staff member of one of Idaho’s US Senators. The conversation centered around why it was so difficult to get federal spending under control - which clearly has not happened. This staffer remarked to me that when Congress cuts spending, they hear loudly from those who […]

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Socialists’ Milwaukee golden age and the light it sheds now
item.description:
The comparison to Milwaukee’s past Socialist mayors highlights how proposals by current Democratic Socialists are much more radical, even if they’re rhetorically similar.

The post Socialists’ Milwaukee golden age and the light it sheds now appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Unserving ‘the Unhoused’ OUR MAYOR has thrown down the NIMBY gauntlet. She is trying to shame opponents of her plan to build a $3.8 million “homeless services” center downtown, a couple of blocks from the  art museum. She says they selfishly don’t want the center in their backyard but cannot offer an alternative plan. I am Read the full article...

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Ignore the Chicken Littles on Amendment 3: Constitutional Fund Dedications are No Way to Craft a Budget
item.description:
Louisiana voters will consider five constitutional amendments when they got to the polls on May 16. Amendment 3, which makes permanent a teacher pay raise, is probably the most discussed—and the least understood. From heated Facebook posts to mail and texting campaigns, hyperbole about “raiding protected funds” to provide the raises has become a distraction. ...

The post Ignore the Chicken Littles on Amendment 3: Constitutional Fund Dedications are No Way to Craft a Budget appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] AB 1383 Would Gut PEPRA and Worsen California’s Pension Crisis
item.description:
A bill quietly moving through the Legislature would gut California’s 2013 pension reforms and plunge the state back toward the kind of fiscal crisis that forced lawmakers to enact the Public Employees’ Pension Reform Act (PEPRA) in an effort to stabilize a system that was spiraling out of control. AB 1383 (McKinnor) would expand the...

The post AB 1383 Would Gut PEPRA and Worsen California’s Pension Crisis first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] The $60 Million Question: Where Did the CBSE Go?
item.description:
A vanished project, a new name, and $60 million in limbo raise serious...

The post The $60 Million Question: Where Did the CBSE Go? appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Sunlight Over Secrecy: Why HB 763 Matters
item.description:
Louisiana works best when government operates in the open. At the Pelican Institute, that’s a principle we return to again and again because it’s foundational to getting policy right and maintaining trust in government. House Bill 763 by Representative Beth Billings is a strong step in that direction, and it’s encouraging to see the House...

The post Sunlight Over Secrecy: Why HB 763 Matters appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.description:

item.title: [Mspolicy] From Classroom to Culture: Teaching the Ideas That Make Societies Prosper
item.description:
While others debate these ideas from a distance, we’re in the room teaching them. This semester, Douglas Carswell was invited to serve as a guest lecturer at the Declaration Center for Freedom Studies at the University of Mississippi—spending the semester working directly with students on the principles that make societies prosper. Week after week, he […]

The post From Classroom to Culture: Teaching the Ideas That Make Societies Prosper appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Boston is 48th out of 55 Metro Areas in the Competition for Young Workers
item.description:
A new analysis from ADP Research (reported in the Wall Street Journal) delivers a clear warning for Massachusetts: the Boston–Cambridge–Newton metropolitan area now ranks 48th out of 55 major U.S. metro areas in attracting young workers. The study tracks more than 400,000 workers in their 20s and evaluates metro areas on a simple but decisive set of factors—job availability, affordability, and access to degree-requiring work. On those measures, Boston lands near the bottom. High wages are no longer enough to offset the region’s high cost structure and weaker hiring momentum.

The post Boston is 48th out of 55 Metro Areas in the Competition for Young Workers appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Investing in the Next Generation of Mississippi Leaders
item.description:
In April, we had the opportunity to welcome students from Pearl River County High School’s Student Council to our office during their visit to Jackson. As part of a broader trip to tour the State Capitol and meet with legislators, the group made time to sit down with Douglas Carswell and our team to learn […]

The post Investing in the Next Generation of Mississippi Leaders appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] From Reform to Expansion: Connecticut’s Evolving Noncompete Proposal
item.description:
What began as a targeted effort to limit noncompete agreements for lower-wage workers is quietly evolving into something considerably broader.  Noncompete agreements — contracts that restrict where employees can work […]

The post From Reform to Expansion: Connecticut’s Evolving Noncompete Proposal  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Fuel Prices Need Abundance
item.description: Kansas families need lower fuel costs through more supply, less inflation, and fewer government-made costs. Fuel prices hit families fast. A gallon of gas is not just a number on a sign. It is the cost of getting to work, taking kids to school, delivering goods, that long-planned summer road trip, and keeping rural Kansas […]

item.title: [Empirecenter] How Pension ‘Spiking’ Drives Up Costs for New York Taxpayers
item.description:
Each year, hundreds of newly retired government workers across New York garner a benefit that would be unheard-of in the....

The post How Pension ‘Spiking’ Drives Up Costs for New York Taxpayers appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Beacontn] Monumental Wins, Unfinished Business: Tennessee’s 2026 Legislative Session and the Road Ahead
item.description: On Thursday, April 23, 2026, the 114th Tennessee General Assembly adjourned sine die. From efforts to regulate data centers and pharmacy benefit managers, to a framework for state takeover of Tennessee’s lowest-performing school districts, legislators saw their fair share of robust policy debates this year.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Legislature Passes Transparency for Federal Funding
item.description:
Federal funds aren’t “free”—they come with strings attached, can drive higher state spending, and are increasingly unstable given Washington’s fiscal position and growing national debt. Earlier in the session, HSB 764 proposed a stronger approach with upfront reporting, full transparency, and legislative approval of major federal funds, ensuring lawmakers could evaluate commitments before they were made. Lawmakers ultimately passed HF 2800, which improves transparency by requiring notification of large or state-matched federal funds—an important step forward, but one that stops short of full oversight and approval.

The post Legislature Passes Transparency for Federal Funding appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Two labor law updates for the 21st century
item.description:
Remote work, flex time and independent contracting have reshaped the American workforce, creating new opportunities for both employers and employees. But in some states, including New Hampshire, outdated labor laws create obstacles to innovation, lost opportunities for employees and unnecessary costs for employers.  To modernize New Hampshire labor laws, legislators are addressing two outdated statutory […]

The post Two labor law updates for the 21st century appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] UT Austin’s New Civic Leadership School Takes Aim at “Anti-Civic Education”
item.description:
A recent episode of Parent Empowerment with Mandy Drogin offered a pointed look at the University of Texas at Austin’s new School of Civic Leadership and the broader fight over what American students should be taught about Western civilization, religion, economics, and the nation’s founding ideals. Guest Justin Dyer, dean of the school, outlined the...

The post UT Austin’s New Civic Leadership School Takes Aim at “Anti-Civic Education” first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Chino Valley Unified Moves to Dissolve Court Injunction Following SCOTUS Mirabelli Ruling on Parental Rights
item.description:
SAN BERNARDINO, CA — The Chino Valley Unified School District (CVUSD) has filed a motion to dissolve a 2024 court-ordered injunction that blocked the district’s parental notification policy. Following the U.S. Supreme Court’s recent decision in Mirabelli v. Bonta, Chino Valley is going back to court to remove the original injunction against it. In March,...

The post Chino Valley Unified Moves to Dissolve Court Injunction Following SCOTUS Mirabelli Ruling on Parental Rights first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Can Oil Industry Lawsuits Compel Rational Energy Policy?
item.description:
When asked in a recent interview why California has the highest gasoline prices in the nation, Jodie Muller, the President of the Western States Petroleum Association, began by stating the following: “You can’t point a finger at one particular person, because, unfortunately, it is decades of policies layered on top of one another. You have local air...

The post Can Oil Industry Lawsuits Compel Rational Energy Policy? first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Wisconsin Gubernatorial Candidates Share Their Vision for the State
item.description:
Wisconsin Gubernatorial Candidates Share Their Vision for the State What’s Next for Wisconsin? Candidates for Governor Weigh In As Wisconsin prepares for its next gubernatorial election, voters deserve a clear understanding of where candidates stand on the issues that matter most. That’s why our organization sent a detailed questionnaire to every declared candidate for governor […]

The post Wisconsin Gubernatorial Candidates Share Their Vision for the State appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Columbia’s First Charter School Approved for 2027 Opening
item.description:
The Missouri Board of Education approved the first charter school in Columbia earlier this month. Set to open in the fall of 2027, the school, sponsored by St. Louis University, […]

The post Columbia’s First Charter School Approved for 2027 Opening appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Pioneer Study: Seniors Paid More for Key Drugs Despite Federal Price Controls
item.description:
Out-of-pocket costs rose in 2025 for widely used Medicare drugs targeted under Inflation Reduction Act  BOSTON – Many Medicare seniors paid higher out-of-pocket costs in 2025 for widely used prescription drugs—despite a federal law intended to lower them, according to a new Pioneer...

The post Pioneer Study: Seniors Paid More for Key Drugs Despite Federal Price Controls  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: James Briggs: The New Journalism WE DON’T ENJOY discouraging a fellow journalist. First of all, nobody cares. It is “inside baseball,” as an editor of mine liked to say. Our members have more important things to do. Still, any job has standards and after some decades in the business (note that I didn’t say “profession”) I have a Read the full article...

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Iowa Voters Will Decide Whether Raising Taxes Should Be Harder
item.description:
Iowa lawmakers approved a proposed constitutional amendment that would require a two-thirds vote of both legislative chambers to raise income taxes, giving voters the final say this November. The amendment is designed to place a higher barrier between taxpayers and tax increases by ensuring higher taxes cannot become the automatic response to budget pressures. The proposal reflects the long-standing vision of late ITR founder David M. Stanley, who believed constitutional taxpayer protections were necessary because government will always try to spend more and tax more unless meaningful limits are put in place.

The post Iowa Voters Will Decide Whether Raising Taxes Should Be Harder appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Report: Several Wausau Schools Among Wisconsin’s Very Best
item.description:
For Immediate Release:  May 7, 2026 Contact: Press@ReformingGoverment.org   IRG Report: Several Wausau Schools Among Wisconsin’s Very Best Jefferson Elementary, Newman Catholic recognized for student success   Delafield, Wis. – The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) released on May 7 “Wausau Wins: How Wausau Became a Wisconsin Education Leader,” a report highlighting the city’s standout […]

The post IRG Report: Several Wausau Schools Among Wisconsin’s Very Best appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

Homeownership is a pillar of the American dream. Unfortunately, many young people see this as unattainable. While blame...

item.description:

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] The American experiment: Separation of powers
item.description: As America prepares for its 250th birthday, it’s important to remember why we celebrate.

item.title: [Beacontn] Beacon Poll: May 2026 Results
item.description: Today, we released the latest installment of the Beacon Poll, a statewide survey of 1,200 Tennessee voters featuring questions about voters’ views on energy sources and production in Tennessee, the condition of roads and what revenue sources could be used to improve them, and political approval ratings at the state and federal level.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Reflections on the May Day demonstrations from a native Chicagoan
item.description:
Growing up in Chicago, May Day was always something discussed in my history classes but was never taught as a positive event, or something to be repeated. 

The post Reflections on the May Day demonstrations from a native Chicagoan appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Help From Above: Can Drone Technology Protect Schools During Emergencies?
item.description:
The safety of children has quickly become one of the highest values in technology policy debates. Few discussions of social media or artificial intelligence policies are complete without a consideration of their impact on the wellbeing of young people. Drone technology has entered this world, as developers work to make drones one of the resources...

The post Help From Above: Can Drone Technology Protect Schools During Emergencies? appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.description:
The Wisconsin Legislature’s “Socialist Caucus” drafted a bill that would add a 17.7% top bracket for households earning $1 million.

item.description:
The Wisconsin Department of Administration has spent nearly $2 million operating two state offices that have since 2019 been denied funding by the Legislature.

item.title: [Mackinac] For once, Michigan said no to new business subsidies
item.description: Now the state should go further

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Chicago business activity returns to contraction
item.description:
The area resumed a long decline in April after three months of modest expansion.

The post Chicago business activity returns to contraction appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Taxpayers Come First as Iowa Wraps Up 2026 Legislative Session
item.description:
Iowa lawmakers advanced major taxpayer-focused reforms during the 2026 session, including a 2% cap on many local property tax levies, school funding reforms, a larger Homestead Exemption, and other changes projected to save taxpayers more than $4 billion over six years. The legislature also prioritized conservative budgeting by limiting state spending growth to 1.4% and advanced a constitutional amendment requiring a two-thirds legislative vote to increase the income tax, giving voters the opportunity to strengthen long-term taxpayer protections. Lawmakers approved additional oversight of federal funds by requiring regular reporting on large federal grants, reflecting growing concerns about national debt, federal instability, and the long-term consequences attached to federal spending.

The post Taxpayers Come First as Iowa Wraps Up 2026 Legislative Session appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] STATEMENT FROM JERSEY1ST, NEW JERSEY POLICY INSTITUTE, AND AMERICANS FOR PROSPERITY–NEW JERSEY ON NEW WORKER CLASSIFICATION RULE

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut Endowment Proposal Raises Pension Costs and Undermines Fiscal Guardrails
item.description:
A new analysis from Yankee Institute, in partnership with the Reason Foundation, finds that Connecticut’s Early Childhood Education Endowment (ECEE) diverts surplus revenues away from pension debt reduction, undermining the fiscal guardrails that have […]

The post Connecticut Endowment Proposal Raises Pension Costs and Undermines Fiscal Guardrails appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] The Recovery of the American Mind: Why Suicidal Empathy Is the Next Conservative Frontier
item.description:
It was a particular pleasure this week to welcome Gad Saad and his wife to Mississippi. Gad is one of the most important conservative thinkers writing today. His new book, Suicidal Empathy: Dying to Be Kind, is published next month, and I am the proud owner of a signed copy. Gad Saad's new book is […]

The post The Recovery of the American Mind: Why Suicidal Empathy Is the Next Conservative Frontier appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.description:
No child should be denied medical care or the love of a caring family simply because of their skin color,..

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut’s Warehouse Mandate Is Getting National Attention — for the Wrong Reasons
item.description:
In March, Connecticut enacted one of the country’s most aggressive warehouse workplace laws, tucked inside an emergency-certified omnibus package rushed through the legislature during a special session.  The U.S. Chamber […]

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] One in Eight UCSD Students Are Placed into Remedial Math: Here’s What One Had to Say About It
item.description:
A while back, I wrote about a report out of UC San Diego (UCSD) about its students’ struggles with basic math. The report focuses on a remedial math course UCSD […]

The post One in Eight UCSD Students Are Placed into Remedial Math: Here’s What One Had to Say About It appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Teacher Appreciation Should Not Be Tied to Spending on Education
item.description: This week is Teacher Appreciation Week across the country, and it prompted Governor Brad Little to issue a press release where he had this to say: “This is Teacher Appreciation Week, a meaningful opportunity to recognize the people who stand at the front of our classrooms each day, shaping the future of our state. I […]

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] "Mom, I hope we can afford my school" -Idaho’s school choice critics ignore the children being helped
item.description: We currently know that roughly half of participating families fall below the program’s priority threshold of 300% of the federal poverty level. What we do not know is the median income of all participating families. But critics routinely leap from “we don’t yet know” to “wealthy families are exploiting the system.”

item.title: [Will Law] Judge Grants WILL’s Emergency Order, Protecting Milwaukee Food Trucks
item.description:
A Milwaukee County judge granted a temporary restraining order blocking enforcement of Milwaukee’s 10:00 p.m. food truck curfew until at least June 10 after WILL challenged the ordinance on behalf of The Fatty Patty owner Abdallah Ismail.

The post Judge Grants WILL’s Emergency Order, Protecting Milwaukee Food Trucks appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Winners & Losers: Texas Wins Again, Cell Phones Lose in Dallas and Rubio Scores at the Vatican
item.description:
Every Friday morning, I join the Cardle & Woolley Show on Talk1370 Radio in Austin to announce the week’s Winners & Losers. It remains very tense in the Strait of Hormuz, and the Virginia Supreme Court just struck down the Democrat-leaning redistricting map voters approved last month, so we’ll wait to see how that all shakes out.  Meanwhile, here’s who made the list: WINNER: Cell Phone Ban...

The post Winners & Losers: Texas Wins Again, Cell Phones Lose in Dallas and Rubio Scores at the Vatican first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Greater Boston Chamber Survey: Youth Outmigration Risks Remain Amidst High Housing Costs, Stagnant Job Market
item.description:
Welcome to the May edition of The House Call. This edition breaks down results from the Greater Boston Chamber of Commerce Young Residents Survey, including the appetite for legislative action on housing costs, and previews our new AI-enabled local land use regulation...

The post Greater Boston Chamber Survey: Youth Outmigration Risks Remain Amidst High Housing Costs, Stagnant Job Market appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Warns the WIAA to End Illegal ﻿Race- and Sex-Based Quotas
item.description:
WILL warned the Wisconsin Interscholastic Athletic Association (WIAA) to scrap its illegal race and sex-based quotas. The WIAA, which governs sports for nearly 90,000 Wisconsin students, denies individuals the ability to seek certain WIAA leadership positions based solely on their race or sex.

The post WILL Warns the WIAA to End Illegal ﻿Race- and Sex-Based Quotas appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Portable benefits coming to Wyoming contract workers soon
item.description: It is right that Wyoming legislators chose to make it easier for many of those who power Wyoming’s economy in construction, transportation, health care and other fields to more easily earn a well-paid living with a safety net without having to give up the flexibility of contract work.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Arizona teachers union actively covering up sexual misconduct probe
item.description:
When allegations of sexual misconduct involving students surface in a public school, the response should be immediate and obvious — protect students, uncover the truth and hold accountable anyone who failed to act.

The post Arizona teachers union actively covering up sexual misconduct probe appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] U.S. life expectancy gap doesn’t mean health system is failing
item.description:
America spends more in total and per capita on health care than any other country. So why do we tend to have shorter lifespans than our peers? New data from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention show that U.S. life expectancy hit a record 79 years in 2024. But a newborn in Germany can […]

The post U.S. life expectancy gap doesn’t mean health system is failing appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] The Big, Beautiful Bill is law. Now Arkansas has to get it right.
item.description:
Passed almost one year ago, the famous “One Big Beautiful Bill” contains the most transformational welfare reforms in modern history. […]

The post The Big, Beautiful Bill is law. Now Arkansas has to get it right. first appeared on Opportunity Arkansas.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Motherhood Is a Discipline Worth Celebrating
item.description:
Sometimes I forget how remarkable my mother is. She holds two degrees in different fields, built a successful professional career, and then, by choice, became a homeschool mom to me and my three siblings until we each left for college. Without her attention and dedication, I would not be who I am today. She patiently...

The post Motherhood Is a Discipline Worth Celebrating first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Deflating the controversy over Medicaid work requirements
item.description:
Earlier this month, Nebraska became the first state to implement the Medicaid work requirements established by last year’s One Big Beautiful Bill Act — now renamed by Republicans as the working families tax cuts. Dozens of others are scrambling to follow suit before the January deadline. Democrats and much of the media have portrayed work […]

The post Deflating the controversy over Medicaid work requirements appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] WAOW: Wausau Schools recognized during Teachers Appreciation Week
item.description:
Wausau Schools recognized during Teachers Appreciation Week WAUSAU, Wis. (WAOW) – This week is Teacher Appreciation week, and the non-profit group Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) is applauding Wausau schools. IRG is a non-profit dedicated to making the government better, ensuring kids have good schools to attend. They awarded Newman Catholic, Thomas Jefferson Elementary, and […]

The post WAOW: Wausau Schools recognized during Teachers Appreciation Week appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Public school teachers are supportive of ESAs
item.description:
A spring teacher survey finds strong support for education savings accounts (ESAs) among K-12 educators, including 74 percent of public school teachers, which is consistent with last year’s results. Conducted by Morning Consult on behalf of EdChoice, the April 2026 national survey asked over 1,000 teachers their opinion of ESAs first without any explanation of...

The post Public school teachers are supportive of ESAs appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Minnesota’s Statehood Day and the first schools
item.description:
Happy Statehood Day, Minnesota! Minnesota officially became a state in 1858, making today Minnesota’s 168th birthday. The North Star State’s growth has been full of ups and downs, but one mark of excellence is Minnesota’s historic commitment to public education. (As I’ve written elsewhere, the distinctly American project of public education is one of the...

The post Minnesota’s Statehood Day and the first schools appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Marty Makary Gets Not-So-Subtle Hint He’s Out at FDA
item.description:
President Trump has signed off on a plan to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary, according to multiple reports. This marks the latest high-level shakeup under HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., following last summer’s ouster of CDC Director Susan Monarez and the February removal of Deputy Secretary Jim O’Neill. Dr. Makary, a former Johns Hopkins...

The post Marty Makary Gets Not-So-Subtle Hint He’s Out at FDA appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Johnlocke] SPLC’s attack of federal indictment falls flat
item.description:
Chris Bray writes for the Federalist about a left-wing group’s questionable legal strategy. Charged with federal crimes for allegedly making false statements to banks and making wire transfers in the names of companies that didn’t exist, the Southern Poverty Law Center hired Hunter Biden’s lawyer (among many others) and signaled an Orange Man Bad defense:...

The post SPLC’s attack of federal indictment falls flat appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Red states target Islamist influence on lawmaking
item.description:
Amanda Head writes for JustTheNews.com about an interesting development in Republican-governed states. As President Donald Trump’s second term produces immigration policies, deportations and cultural shifts toward a more concentrated citizen population, red states are passing their own legislation and running candidates focused on thwarting Islamic community developments and reducing their influence.  “This is a major...

The post Red states target Islamist influence on lawmaking appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Omar’s name linked closely to COVID-era fraud scheme
item.description:
Jim Hoft writes for the Gateway Pundit about bad news for a member of the congressional “Squad.” Fresh court exhibits from the massive $250 MILLION Feeding Our Future fraud trial have revealed that Rep. Ilhan Omar’s name appeared MULTIPLE times in email chains and text messages with convicted fraudster Aimee Bock, the mastermind behind the...

item.title: [Pacificresearch] The Gordon Chang Report–China Has Proliferated Nuclear Weapons. What Should America Do?
item.description:
READ THE PDF China Has Proliferated Nuclear Weapons. What Should America Do? “The entire United States is within range of our nuclear weapons, and a nuclear button is always on my desk,” Kim Jong Un, the supreme leader of the Democratic People’s Republic of Korea, boasted in January 2018. “This is reality, not a threat.” […]
